user_query:
I’m preparing a short roundup on local organizers improving access to victim rights resources, and I need your help turning my source files into publishable materials. Please use the three input files under input/ to extract details, rewrite a blurb, and draft two emails. 1) Extract structured event details from both HTML pages at input/web/city_coalition_press_release.html and input/web/county_advocacy_event.html and save a single JSON array to output/data/events.json. For each event, include exactly these fields (strings unless noted): title, date_text (human-readable text inside the tag), date_iso (the datetime attribute of the tag), time_text, venue, address, organizer_name, organizer_title, organization, contact_email, quote_text, quote_speaker (the person named after “said”), supporting_note (set to the text of any

if present, otherwise null). Preserve the text as it appears in the HTML (no paraphrasing). 2) Rewrite the blurb in input/notes/newsletter_blurb.txt and save to output/writing/newsletter_blurb_rewritten.txt. Requirements: neutral, survivor-centered tone; avoid sensational language; maximum 120 words; must mention both organization names exactly as they appear in the HTML files. 3) Draft two emails and save them as Markdown files: - output/emails/editor_pitch.md: Include a Subject line that contains both organization names and both date_text values. Body: 3–5 sentences framing the angle (organizers expanding access to services), include one quote snippet of 20 words or fewer with attribution by last name (e.g., “...,” said Ortiz), and a bullet list with one line per event in this exact shape: "- [Organization]: [date_text] [time_text] — [venue], [address]" (values pulled from output/data/events.json). - output/emails/organizer_request.md: First two lines must be "To: [contact_email from the City Justice Coalition page]" and "Subject: Fact-check request: [title from the City Justice Coalition page]". Body: brief greeting and purpose, followed by a numbered list of the extracted values to confirm (date_iso, date_text, time_text, venue, address, organizer_name, organizer_title, organization, quote_text, contact_email), then a courteous closing. Do not fabricate information; derive all facts from the provided inputs. Create any needed folders so the output paths exist. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
